name: Akamai
description: Monitor Akamai CDN configurations, edge security policies, and API client access

config.setupInstructions:
1. Log in to Akamai Control Center
2. Go to Identity and Access Management
3. Click Create API client
4. Select Quick for basic access or Advanced for specific API permissions
5. Download the .edgerc file
6. Copy the host, client_token, client_secret, and access_token values

Required API permissions:
- Identity Management: read access (for Employee Access check)
- Property Manager: read access (for Infrastructure Inventory check)
- Application Security: read access (for Production Firewall check)

credentialFields.label: API Host
credentialFields.helpText: Your Akamai API host from the .edgerc file (e.g., akab-xxxxx.luna.akamaiapis.net). Do NOT include https://

credentialFields.label: Client Token
credentialFields.helpText: Client token from your .edgerc file (starts with akab-)

credentialFields.label: Access Token
credentialFields.helpText: Access token from your .edgerc file (starts with akab-)

checks.name: Employee Access
checks.description: Verifies API client authentication and retrieves the current user profile to confirm access controls are in place

checks.name: Infrastructure Inventory
checks.description: Lists all Akamai property configurations to provide an inventory of CDN and edge delivery assets

checks.name: Production Firewall
checks.description: Lists Akamai Application Security configurations to verify WAF and edge security policies are in place
